title: Mental Math Multiplication
essential_question: What strategies help you multiply mentally?

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. 25=1/4 of 100, so 4×25=100.

pretest.question: 4 × 99 = ___ (think 4×100 − 4)
hints: Think about what mental math multiplication means.
Try drawing an array or using place value.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 396. Multiply place by place.

pretest.question: Use halving and doubling: 5 × 28 = 10 × 14 = ___
hints: Think about what mental math multiplication means.
Try drawing an array or using place value.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 140. Multiply place by place.
The answer is True.

pretest.question: 8 × 125 = ___ (think 8×100+8×25)
hints: Think about what mental math multiplication means.
Try drawing an array or using place value.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 1000. Multiply place by place.

learn.title: Why Mental Math?
learn.content: Quick mental multiplication saves time and helps you spot mistakes. Use patterns and shortcuts.
learn.tts: Quick mental multiplication saves time and helps you spot mistakes. Use patterns and shortcuts.

learn.title: Friendly Factors
learn.content: Some numbers multiply easily: 4×25=100, 5×20=100, 8×125=1,000. Memorize these friendly pairs.
learn.tts: Some numbers multiply easily: 4×25=100, 5×20=100, 8×125=1,000. Memorize these friendly pairs.

learn.title: Compensation
learn.content: For 6 × 99, think 6×100=600, then subtract 6: 594. Adjust after using a friendlier number.
learn.tts: For 6 × 99, think 6×100=600, then subtract 6: 594. Adjust after using a friendlier number.

learn.title: Halving and Doubling
learn.content: a × b stays the same if you double one and halve the other: 5×28 = 10×14 = 140.
learn.tts: a × b stays the same if you double one and halve the other: 5×28 = 10×14 = 140.

learn.title: Choose the Best Strategy
learn.content: Different numbers suit different tricks. Pick the fastest one.
learn.tts: Different numbers suit different tricks. Pick the fastest one.
